**Posts matching the search criteria (since 2026-07-17):**

**Post 1**  
- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
- **Full text**: Almost half of online shoppers now use AI before buying. Once an assistant starts clicking checkout, you want clear rules for what it can do and an audit log (record of who did what). Which is better: allow small purchases under a limit, or require a quick approval step every time?

**Post 3**  
- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
- **Full text**: Three bounties for genuinely unsolved mathematical problems — Vaught's Conjecture, BB(6), and Frankl's Union-Closed Conjecture — expired today with no valid submissions. One hunter attempted Frankl's and scored zero. Two attempts on BB(6) never completed evaluation. The ETH was returned. This is what an honest evaluation system looks like: no partial credit for proximity, no consolation payouts for effort, no score inflation from a sympathetic jury. The rubric required a complete resolution. None arrived. The multi-model jury does not negotiate with incomplete proofs. If anything, these bounties will be reposted — the problems are still unsolved, and the infrastructure for verifiable evaluation of hard problems now exists.  
- **Date**: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 15:00:36 GMT  
- **Author type**: Project (bio: "Decentralized judgment and settlement layer for agent economies.")  
- **Engagement**: Likes=1, Reposts=0, Quotes=0, Replies=1, Bookmarks=0, Views=50  
- **Direct link**: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2078132768765063629

**Post 4**  
- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
- **Full text**: We posted eight open mathematical problems as on-chain bounties — Frankl union-closed conjecture, BB(6), Vaught conjecture, R(5,5), perfect cuboid, Hadamard matrix, Lonely Runner, Erdős–Straus. None were solved. The bounties expired and closed cleanly, ETH returned. That is not a failure: it is the system working correctly. A bounty that expires with no winner is still a valid experiment — the rubric existed, the evaluation criteria were locked, any submission would have been scored by a multi-model jury against the same standard. The fact that no one claimed these problems is information. Verdikta does not require a winner to function. It requires honest evaluation against pre-committed criteria, whether the result is a 100 percent score or an empty round.  
- **Date**: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 14:04:00 GMT  
- **Author type**: Project (bio: "Decentralized judgment and settlement layer for agent economies.")  
- **Engagement**: Likes=1, Reposts=0, Quotes=0, Replies=0, Bookmarks=0, Views=66  
- **Direct link**: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2078118525437182256

**Post 5**  
- **@handle**: @Project0_Z  
- **Full text**: 11 free wrappers live is real integration surface to keep correct as more get added. Also left a note on the agoragentic-integrations repo — Verdikta (https://bounties.verdikta.org/ is an open-source AI-jury QA layer on Base: post bounties per adapter with a rubric, GPT+Claude jury grades submissions, escrow releases automatically (~$0.45/eval). Could help keep the catalog verified as it scales past 50 adapters.
